data.title: Las Vegas Sands Reports Higher Profit in 1Q
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Las Vegas Sands reported a jump in profit in the first quarter.

The resorts company on Wednesday reported a profit of $567 million, or 85 cents a share, That compares with its profit of $352 million, or 49 cents a share a year earlier.

Stripping out certain one-time items, the company logged adjusted earnings of 91 cents a share. Analysts surveyed by FactSet were expecting 76 cents a share.

Net revenue rose to $3.59 billion, up from $2.86 billion a year prior. Analysts were expecting $3.36 billion.

Casino revenue rose to $2.74 billion from $2.13 billion a year earlier. Rooms revenue grew to $377 million from $324 million, food and beverage revenue rose to $176 million from $141 million, and mall revenue rose to $204 million from $186 million.

Revenue from the company's Macao operations rose to $2.11 billion, up from $1.71 billion a year earlier.

"We continued to execute our strategic objectives during the quarter as we delivered growth in both Singapore and Macao while continuing to increase the return of capital to shareholders," Chief Executive Patrick Dumont said.

Shares rose 2% to $58.00 in after-hours trading Wednesday. The stock closed down 1.2% at $56.85, down 13% year to date.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: United Rentals Inc. Profit Climbs In Q1
data.description:
WASHINGTON (dpa-AFX) - United Rentals Inc. (URI) announced earnings for its first quarter that Increases, from the same period last year

The company's earnings came in at $531 million, or $8.43 per share. This compares with $518 million, or $7.91 per share, last year.

Excluding items, United Rentals Inc. reported adjusted earnings of $9.71 per share for the period.

The company's revenue for the period rose 7.3% to $3.98 billion from $3.71 billion last year.

United Rentals Inc. earnings at a glance (GAAP) :

-Earnings: $531 Mln. vs. $518 Mln. last year.

-EPS: $8.43 vs. $7.91 last year.

-Revenue: $3.98 Bln vs. $3.71 Bln last year.

-Guidance:

Full year revenue guidance: $ 16.9 B To $ 17.4 B

data.title: Selective Insurance Group Inc reports results for the quarter ended March 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Selective Insurance Group Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of $1.69​​ per share for the quarter ended March 31, lower than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of $1.76. The mean expectation of eight analysts for the quarter was for earnings of $1.86 per share. Wall Street expected results to range from $1.25 to $2.10 per share.

  • Revenue fell 1.2% to $1.23 billion from a year ago; analysts expected $1.31 billion.

  • Selective Insurance Group Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was $1.58​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $95.4 million.

  • Selective Insurance Group Inc shares had risen by 7.3% this quarter and lost 3.3% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 4.7% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, two analysts negatively revised earnings estimates

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "hold" and the breakdown of recommendations is 2 "strong buy" or "buy," 5 "hold" and 1 "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the property & casualty insurance peer group is "buy."

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Selective Insurance Group Inc is $86.00, about 6% above its last closing price of $80.88

data.title: Packaging Corp of America beats Q1 profit estimates as demand improves
data.description:
Overview

  • US packaging producer's Q1 sales rose yr/yr but missed analyst expectations

  • Adjusted EPS for Q1 beat analyst expectations

  • Company cites favorable prices, lower fiber costs, and improved mix as drivers of earnings

Outlook

  • Packaging Corp expects Q2 EPS of $2.33, excluding special items

  • Company sees strong Packaging segment demand and higher corrugated volume in Q2

  • Company expects higher prices for containerboard, corrugated, and paper products in Q2

Result Drivers

  • FAVORABLE PRICES AND MIX - Co said higher prices and improved mix in legacy packaging and paper segments contributed to earnings growth

  • LOWER FIBER AND OPERATING COSTS - Lower fiber cost and reduced labor and operating expenses in the legacy packaging business supported results

  • HIGHER FREIGHT COSTS AND GREIF LOSS - Higher freight costs and a loss from the acquired Greif operations offset some gains, with Greif loss attributed to lower volume and higher costs from a winter storm

data.title: TriplePoint Venture Growth BDC Corp. to Announce 2026 First Quarter Financial Results on Wednesday, May 6, 2026
data.description:
MENLO PARK, Calif.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--April 22, 2026--

TriplePoint Venture Growth BDC Corp. (the "Company"), a leading financing provider to venture growth stage companies backed by a select group of venture capital firms in technology and other high growth industries, today announced it will release its financial results for its first quarter ended March 31, 2026 after market-close on Wednesday, May 6, 2026. James P. Labe, chief executive officer and chairman of the board, Sajal K. Srivastava, president and chief investment officer, and Mike L. Wilhelms, chief financial officer, will host a conference call that same day at 5:00 p.m., Eastern Time to discuss the Company's financial results.

To listen to the call, investors and analysts should dial (844) 826-3038 (domestic) or +1 (412) 317-5184 (international) and ask to join the TriplePoint Venture Growth BDC Corp. call. Please dial in at least five minutes before the scheduled start time. A replay of the call will be available through June 6, 2026, by dialing (855) 669-9658 (domestic) or +1 (412) 317-0088 (international) and entering conference ID 5704060.

The conference call also will be available via a live audio webcast in the investor relations section of the Company's website, https://www.tpvg.com. An online archive of the webcast will be available on the Company's website for one year after the call.

ABOUT TRIPLEPOINT VENTURE GROWTH BDC CORP.

TriplePoint Venture Growth BDC Corp. is an externally-managed business development company focused on providing customized debt financing with warrants and direct equity investments primarily to venture growth stage companies in technology and other high growth industries backed by a select group of venture capital firms. The Company's sponsor, TriplePoint Capital, is a Sand Hill Road-based global investment platform which provides customized debt financing, leasing, direct equity investments and other complementary solutions to venture capital-backed companies in technology and other high growth industries at every stage of their development with unparalleled levels of creativity, flexibility and service. For more information about TriplePoint Venture Growth BDC Corp., visit https://www.tpvg.com. For more information about TriplePoint Capital, visit https://www.triplepointcapital.com.

FORWARD-LOOKING STATEMENTS

Statements included herein may constitute "forward-looking statements," which relate to future events or our future performance or financial condition. These statements are not guarantees of future performance, condition or results and involve a number of risks and uncertainties. Actual results and conditions may differ materially from those in the forward-looking statements as a result of a number of factors, including those described from time to time in our filings with the SEC. The Company undertakes no duty to update any forward-looking statements made herein.

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260422291110/en/

CONTACT:

INVESTOR RELATIONS AND MEDIA CONTACT

The IGB Group

Leon Berman

212-477-8438

data.title: Kaiser Aluminum Raises FY26 Outlook; Posts Higher 1Q Rev, Profit
data.description:
By Christopher Kuo

Kaiser Aluminum raised its outlook amid higher shipments and an increase in its average realized sales price during the first quarter.

The aluminum manufacturer on Wednesday raised its full-year outlook for adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization, now expecting Ebitda to improve by 20% to 30% year over year.

Kaiser said its improved outlook is due to stronger demand, favorable pricing, an improved mix within its packaging operations, as well as consistent execution across its portfolio, along with metal lag gains, referring to the differences in the current cost of metal purchases and the period in which sales are realized.

The company reported a profit of $62.5 million, or $3.71 a share, compared with $21.6 million, or $1.31 a share, a year earlier.

Adjusted earnings were $3.74 a share. Analysts polled by FactSet were expecting earnings of $1.96 a share.

The company's Ebitda growth was driven by higher value packaging mix, improving aerospace demand, widening scrap spreads and strong customer activity across all end markets, said Chief Executive Keith Harvey.

Revenue rose to $1.12 billion from $777.4 million a year earlier, boosted by higher shipments and an increase in average realized sales prices. Analysts were expecting revenue of $1 billion.

Shipments for the first quarter grew 7% year over year, the company said.

Write to Christopher Kuo at chris.kuo@wsj.com

data.title: Pineapple Financial expands share buyback to $15M, immediate repurchases to commence
data.description:
Pineapple Financial approved an expanded $15 million share repurchase program and will commence repurchases immediately under the initial authorization.

Key Highlights:

  • Board increased buyback authorization from $3.0M to up to $15.0M of common shares.
  • Repurchases to begin immediately under the existing $3.0M authorization, subject to laws and absence of material non-public info.
  • Additional up to $12.0M may be repurchased over time, subject to further Board approval and solvency requirements.
  • Timing, price and amount of repurchases at company discretion; program may be modified, suspended or terminated.

Original SEC Filing: Pineapple Financial Inc. [ PAPL ] - 8-K - Apr. 22, 2026

data.title: Oceaneering Reports Q1 2026: Revenue $692.4M, Operating Income $57.8M, Net Income $36.1M
data.description:
Oceaneering reported first-quarter 2026 results with revenue of $692.4 million and operating income of $57.8 million, compared with $674.5 million and $73.5 million a year earlier. Net income was $36.1 million and adjusted EBITDA was $83.7 million; diluted EPS was $0.36 and adjusted diluted EPS was $0.30. The company noted strong Aerospace and Defense Technologies activity, $1 billion of orders in the quarter and maintained full-year EBITDA guidance of $390 million to $440 million.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $692.4 million for Q1 2026, up 3% year-over-year (Q1 2025: $674.5 million).
  • Gross profit: $127.3 million (gross margin shown in statement; gross profit figure provided in release).
  • Operating income: $57.8 million for Q1 2026, down 21% year-over-year (Q1 2025: $73.5 million).
  • Net income: $36.1 million for Q1 2026, down 28% year-over-year (Q1 2025: $50.4 million).
  • Adjusted EBITDA: $83.7 million for Q1 2026, a 13% decrease versus Q1 2025; diluted EPS $0.36, adjusted diluted EPS $0.30.

Business Highlights

  • Orders and backlog: Generated approximately $1 billion of total orders in Q1 2026, including over $300 million in Subsea Robotics awards and $175 million in Aerospace and Defense Technologies awards; Manufactured Products backlog at $492 million as of March 31, 2026.
  • Subsea Robotics (SSR): Revenue increased to $214.3 million, while ROV utilization declined to 61% (down from 67% a year earlier); ROV revenue per day utilized rose to $12,401 reflecting pricing mix and discrete first-quarter items.
  • Manufactured Products: Revenue up 6% with operating income rising to $26.1 million and margin expanding to 18%, driven by higher‑margin backlog execution and strong Rotator valve performance; 12‑month book-to-bill of 0.91.
  • Offshore Projects Group (OPG): Revenue declined 18% reflecting typical seasonality and lower international activity; operating income decreased and margin fell to 14%.
  • Integrity Management & Digital Solutions (IMDS) and ADTech: IMDS faced lower volumes in West Africa and Australia and Middle East activity was flat due to regional conflict; ADTech revenue grew 35% to $131.2 million with increased submarine repair and maintenance activity but operating income impacted by an accrual related to a contract dispute.

Original SEC Filing: OCEANEERING INTERNATIONAL INC [ OII ] - 8-K - Apr. 22, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Maze Therapeutics prices $150M registered offering; expects ~$144.7M net proceeds, funds into 2029
data.description:
Maze Therapeutics priced a registered offering of 5,540,000 shares and 850,000 pre-funded warrants, expected gross proceeds of $150.0 million.

Key Highlights:

  • Offering: 5,540,000 shares at $23.50 and 850,000 pre-funded warrants at $23.499 each.
  • Net proceeds: Company estimates approximately $144.7 million after underwriting discounts and expenses.
  • Use of proceeds: Primarily advance R&D for MZE829 (APOL1-mediated kidney disease) and MZE782 (PKU, CKD); general corporate purposes.
  • Cash runway: Combined with existing cash and securities, proceeds are expected to fund operations into 2029 under current plan.
  • Underwriter and timing: Leerink Partners sole underwriter; offering expected to close on or about April 23, 2026, subject to customary conditions.

Original SEC Filing: Maze Therapeutics, Inc. [ MAZE ] - 8-K - Apr. 22, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Jabil Board keeps John Plant and Tiger Tyagarajan after rejecting resignations
data.description:
Jabil retained directors John Plant and Tiger Tyagarajan after rejecting their conditional resignations following the 2026 annual meeting vote.

Key Highlights:

  • The Nominating and Corporate Governance Committee recommended rejecting the resignations of Plant and Tyagarajan; the Board concurred.
  • Both directors had not received majority votes at the 2026 meeting but did not participate in the committee’s review or Board decision.
  • Board cited attendance (over 75% in fiscal 2026 to date), engagement, qualifications and contributions in deciding to retain them.
  • Plant and Tyagarajan will continue serving on the Board and committees through the 2027 annual meeting unless they resign or are removed.

Original SEC Filing: JABIL INC [ JBL ] - 8-K - Apr. 22, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Contango Announces $9.0M Peak Gold JV Distribution, $63M+ 2026 Exploration Program
data.description:
Contango received a $9.0 million cash distribution from the Peak Gold JV and outlined a fully funded, multi‑project 2026 exploration and infrastructure program.

Key Highlights:

  • Peak Gold JV paid Contango $9,000,000 in cash on March 25, 2026, providing near‑term liquidity.
  • Lucky Shot: 5,900m underground drilling completed; additional 12,100m planned as part of a $21M spend toward a H1 2027 Feasibility Study.
  • Johnson Tract: $17M 2026 budget for 3‑mile access road, camp winterization, portal prep and FAST‑41 environmental permitting.
  • Kitsault Valley: New MRE due end of Q2 and a planned 40,000m surface drill program starting in June as part of a $25M campaign.

Original SEC Filing: Contango Silver & Gold Inc. [ CTGO ] - 8-K - Apr. 22, 2026

data.title: Oceaneering Files 8K - Operations And Financial Condition >OII
data.description:
Oceaneering International Inc. (OII) filed a Form 8K - Operations and Financial Condition - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on April 22, 2026.

On April 22, 2026, Oceaneering International, Inc. ("Oceaneering" or "we") issued a press release announcing Oceaneering's earnings for the first quarter ended March 31, 2026. A copy of that press release is furnished as Exhibit 99.1 to this report and is incorporated by reference into this item 2.02.

The information furnished pursuant to this Item 2.02, including Exhibit 99.1, shall not be deemed to be "filed" for the purposes of Section 18 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the "Exchange Act"), or otherwise subject to the liabilities of that section, nor shall such information be deemed incorporated by reference into any registration statement or other filing under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, or the Exchange Act, unless specifically identified in such filing as being incorporated by reference in such filing.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/73756/000007375626000080/oii-20260422.htm

Any exhibits and associated documents for this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/73756/000007375626000080/0000073756-26-000080-index.htm

Public companies must file a Form 8-K, or current report, with the SEC generally within four days of any event that could materially affect a company's financial position or the value of its shares.
